title: Cotgrave Place W.5365 Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed; 2 survived (Sgt Watkins and F/S H.W. Gill)
memorial_type: Memorial plaque with 12 Squadron fox-head badge
narrative: In February 1941, B Flight of 12 (Bomber) Squadron was dispersing from RAF Binbrook in Lincolnshire to nearby RAF Tollerton, south of Nottingham. The Wellington was on a local familiarisation flight. During the flight, the aircraft suffered two separate control incidents, nearly diving into the ground on each occasion. The pilot, Squadron Leader Lawrence, managed to recover each time but was unable to avoid striking an oak tree while attempting to land back at Tollerton. The Wellington crashed near Cotgrave Place manor house. Seven men were killed and two survived. One survivor, F/Sgt H.W. Gill, was saved by being in the rear gunner's turret rather than his usual navigator position.

crew.name: Phillip Cutliffe Lawrence
crew.fate_detail: Killed when Wellington W5365 struck an oak tree near Tollerton and crashed at Cotgrave Place.

crew.name: Robert Atkinson
crew.fate_detail: Killed in the crash at Cotgrave Place.

crew.name: Robert Tod Tomlinson
crew.fate_detail: Sgt Tomlinson was awarded the DFM for rescuing the pilot from the burning aircraft.

crew.name: Watkins
crew.fate_detail: One of two survivors of the crash.

crew.name: H.W. Gill
crew.fate_detail: Survived because he was positioned in the rear gunner's turret rather than his usual navigator seat. His unusual positioning saved his life.

cause_of_loss: Control problems during flight; struck oak tree attempting to return to Tollerton
operation_type: Local familiarisation flight for squadron dispersal
memorial_details: A memorial plaque mounted on a plinth at the first tee of Nottinghamshire Golf and Country Club (formerly Cotgrave Place). Features the 12 Squadron fox-head badge.

external_narratives.summary: Memorial plaque at first tee of golf club, featuring 12 Squadron fox-head badge.
Records seven killed, two survived. Wellington II variant.
